question_text: Describe what happens during total internal reflection and state one condition needed for it to occur.
correct_answer: Light reflects completely off a surface (does not escape into the other medium). This occurs when light travels from a denser medium to a less dense medium and hits the boundary at an angle greater than the critical angle.
explanation: Total internal reflection is when all the light bounces back instead of passing through or being refracted. It only happens when light tries to escape from a denser material (like glass) to a less dense one (like air) at a steep enough angle.
